title: The Kernel and Processor Modes
summary: Explains user mode vs. kernel mode, mode switching via interrupts, kernel functions (process/memory/file/IPC management), and why mode separation protects the system.
content_points: User mode: restricted access (memory zones limited, no hardware access).
Kernel mode: unrestricted – the OS runs here.
Switching user → kernel: software interrupt or exception. Kernel → user: interrupt return.
No application code runs in kernel mode → application errors cannot harm the OS or other apps.
Kernel functions: process management, memory management, file system management, IPC.
learning_objectives: Distinguish user mode and kernel mode
List the main functions of the kernel
